parallel_execution.motivation: Execution at Layer-1 is structurally constrained by a globally shared state.

horizontal_partitioning.motivation: No native sharding mechanism is included in the adopted Layer-1 baseline.

absence_of_global_serialization.motivation: Global ordering is required across the ledger.

scalable_coordination_model.motivation: Consensus requires global coordination across validators.

ps_scalability.source: Derived from Ethereum Layer-1 architecture and official scaling documentation

absence_of_single_entity_unilateral_stat.motivation: No single entity can unilaterally control valid ledger state in Ethereum’s native operational model.

structural_requirement_for_multi_entity_.motivation: Validation structurally requires distributed validator participation.

validator_admission_independence.motivation: Validator participation is not controlled by a discretionary central authority.

absence_of_structurally_fixed_validator_.motivation: The validator set is not structurally closed.

ags_control_distribution_governance.source: Ethereum Foundation, Staking and Proof-of-Stake documentation

native_privilege_differentiation_capabil.motivation: Ethereum does not provide native role-based privilege differentiation comparable to enterprise identity governance systems.

native_identity_lifecycle_governance_cap.motivation: Ethereum does not include a native identity lifecycle governance layer.

structural_independence_of_identity_gove.motivation: No native distributed identity governance framework is built into the protocol.

non_unilateral_compromise_possible.motivation: A single validator cannot unilaterally compromise the system under the native Ethereum model.

majority_compromise_required.motivation: Relevant attacks require majority stake control.

supermajority_compromise_required.motivation: Finality-related compromise requires supermajority consensus thresholds.

eco_baseline_footprint.formula: BIF = (vCPU x 15 EUR) + (Storage GB x 0.08 EUR)
eco_baseline_footprint.source: Derived from MCC (4 vCPU, 2000 GB) and normalized AWS-based cost model

eco_variable_transaction_cost.source: Derived from 30-day average transaction fee using Etherscan Average Transaction Fee Chart

cryptographic_linkage_between_records.motivation: Blocks and transactions are linked through cryptographic hashes.

independent_integrity_verification.motivation: Full nodes and validators can independently verify integrity and validity of state transitions.

rci_tamper_evidence_auditability.source: Ethereum Foundation, Nodes and Clients documentation

protocol_enforced_irreversible_commit.motivation: Ethereum provides protocol-native PoS finality through its finality layer.

multi_party_confirmation_requirement.motivation: Finality requires distributed validator confirmation rather than unilateral acceptance.

absence_of_unilateral_rollback_capabilit.motivation: Rollback is not structurally achievable by a single actor under the native protocol model.

absence_of_single_writer_requirement.motivation: The native Ethereum operational model does not rely on a single structurally privileged writer.

quorum_based_write_continuation.motivation: Write continuation depends on distributed consensus rather than on a single primary node.

automatic_write_continuation_mechanism.motivation: The native protocol continues operation without centralized manual failover.

multi_node_write_survivability.motivation: The native validator-based model preserves write continuity through distributed participation.
